chunks.en: The thing I always grab before leaving home is my company laptop and its charger.
chunks.ja: 家を出る前に必ず手に取るのは、会社のノートパソコンとその充電器です。

chunks.en: The way I check I didn't forget anything is a quick pat on my bag and pockets.
chunks.ja: 忘れ物がないか確認する私のやり方は、カバンとポケットをさっと手で触ってみることです。

chunks.en: When I'm running late, I rush out the door and just check my phone and keys.
chunks.ja: 遅刻しそうなときは、ドアから慌てて飛び出して、スマホと鍵だけを確認します。

chunks.en: The one thing I often forget is my ID card for entering the office building.
chunks.ja: 私がよく忘れるものは、オフィスビルに入るための社員証です。

outline: Opening: what I always grab before leaving
Point 1: how I check for forgotten items
Point 2: rushing out when I'm late
Wrap-up: the one thing I often forget

modelTalk.text:
Before I leave home, I always grab the same three things. My phone, my keys, and my wallet. I touch each one and say them in my head. Phone, keys, wallet. It's a quick little check, but it works for me. If I've got all three, I feel calm and ready.

But some mornings I wake up late. My heart starts racing. I rush around and pull on my shoes. I grab my bag and run out the door. On those days, I don't check anything. I just go.

So of course, I forget stuff. The thing I leave behind most is my umbrella. I set it by the door, and then I forget it. Later it rains, and I get wet. Next time I'll remember. Well, maybe.

chunks.en: The thing I always grab before heading out is my work laptop and charger.
chunks.ja: 出かける前に必ず手に取るのは、仕事用のノートパソコンと充電器です。

chunks.en: The way I check I didn't forget anything is a quick pat on my pockets.
chunks.ja: 忘れ物がないか確認する私のやり方は、ポケットをさっと手で叩くことです。

chunks.en: When I'm running late, I rush out the door and skip breakfast completely.
chunks.ja: 遅刻しそうなときは、ドアから飛び出して朝食を完全に抜きます。

chunks.en: The one thing I often forget is my umbrella, even when it's raining.
chunks.ja: よく忘れる物は傘で、雨が降っているときでさえ忘れます。

outline: Opening: what I always grab before leaving
How I check for forgotten items
When I'm late and rush out
Wrap-up: the thing I often forget

modelTalk.text:
Okay, so before I leave home, I always grab a few things. My phone comes first. Then my wallet and my keys. I put them in the same pocket every day. That way I don't have to think too much.

I check everything at the door. I touch my pockets one by one. Phone, keys, wallet, mask. If something's missing, I feel it right away. It's a simple habit, but it works for me.

When I'm running late, it's a different story. I rush around and grab stuff fast. My heart beats quickly. I lock the door and just run. Sometimes I still forget things.

The one thing I always leave behind? My umbrella. I put it down somewhere and walk off. Then it rains, and I get wet. So yeah, I really need to fix that habit soon.
